title: Salix ilectica
taxonomy: Salix ilectica is a species of the genus Salix, which is commonly known as the willow family. It belongs to the family Salicaceae, a group of flowering plants. The Salicaceae family is further classified under the order Malpighiales and the class Magnoliopsida. At the kingdom level, Salix ilectica is classified as Plantae, which includes all plants. This classification follows the taxonomic system of Carl Linnaeus and is widely accepted in the scientific community.
uses: Salix ilectica, also known as glandular willow, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's bark and leaves contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain and reduce fever. Additionally, Salix ilectica has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Salix ilectica.
